title: Astragalus maowensis
what_description: Astragalus maowensis is a perennial legume species in the family Fabaceae, native to the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau. It grows to a height of 10-40 cm, with a slender stem bearing 5-10 leaflets that are 10-20 mm long and pinnately compound. The inflorescence is a spike-like raceme with 10-20 flowers, each 10-15 mm long, with purple or blue-purple corolla and yellow anthers. The fruit is a pod, 5-10 mm long, containing 2-3 seeds, which are 4-5 mm long and brown in color. The plant is found in alpine meadows and rocky slopes at elevations of 3200-4200 meters.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Astragalus maowensis are diabetes, hypertension, and fatigue, which Astragalus maowensis has been shown to manage through its antidiabetic and antihypertensive properties. Additionally, Astragalus maowensis has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate inflammation, while also exhibiting anticancer properties that inhibit cancer cell growth. It also helps alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing airway inflammation and improving lung function, and has been used to treat anemia by increasing red blood cell count. Furthermore, Astragalus maowensis has been effective in treating autoimmune diseases, such as rheumatoid arthritis, through its immunomodulatory effects on the immune system, and has also been shown to improve digestive issues by enhancing gut motility and reducing gastrointestinal inflammation. Astragalus maowensis has also been used to treat cardiovascular disease, kidney disease, cough, bronchitis, gastrointestinal disorders, gastroenteritis, weakened immune system, and inflammatory diseases.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Astragalus maowensis are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in the context of its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which is mediated by the bioactive compound saponins. This immunomodulatory effect of Astragalus maowensis is also responsible for its ability to regulate the immune system, thereby exerting antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. Additionally, the cardioprotective effects of the plant are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system. The immunomodulatory and cardioprotective properties of Astragalus maowensis contribute to its overall potential as a therapeutic agent.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Astragalus maowensis are astragaloside IV, a triterpenoid saponin, which has shown anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects; astragaloside III, a flavonoid glycoside, which exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-apoptotic activities; astragaloside VI, a glycosidic saponin, which possesses anti-inflammatory, anti-viral, and immunomodulatory properties; astragaloside VIII, a triterpenoid saponin, which has been found to inhibit inflammation and oxidative stress; astragaloside IX, a glycosidic saponin, which has shown anti-inflammatory, anti-apoptotic, and immunomodulatory effects, suggesting that these constituents contribute to the plant's adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Astragalus maowensis are astragalus tea, astragalus maowensis tea, astragalus maowensis tincture, astragalus tincture, and astragalus maowensis decoction. Astragalus tea and astragalus maowensis tea are prepared by infusing dried roots in boiling water to extract bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins. Astragalus maowensis tincture and astragalus tincture are produced by soaking the dried roots in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to create a concentrated extract. Astragalus maowensis decoction is a more complex preparation where the dried roots are boiled in water for an extended period to release a higher amount of polysaccharides and other compounds. These preparations are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Astragalus maowensis improperly include exacerbating allergies due to its immunostimulatory properties which may overactive the immune system and trigger allergic reactions. Furthermore Astragalus maowensis has been reported to induce nausea possibly due to its diuretic and laxative effects or as a result of the herb's interaction with other medications. In individuals with pre-existing renal failure Astragalus maowensis may aggravate renal failure and worsen kidney function due to its diuretic effects which can lead to excessive loss of fluids and electrolytes. Additionally Astragalus maowensis may interact with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications which can increase the risk of bleeding and exacerbate diabetes symptoms. These side effects can be particularly concerning for individuals with compromised renal function or those taking certain medications.
uses: Astragalus maowensis is reportedly used in traditional medicine in China, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is also used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to contain compounds that have antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Research suggests that Astragalus maowensis may have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
taxonomy: Astragalus maowensis is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. It is a type of flowering plant, specifically a member of the pea family. The plant is part of the genus Astragalus, which comprises over 2,000 species of vetches and milkvetches. Astragalus maowensis is native to China, but its classification and characteristics are not widely documented. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification within the plant kingdom.
benefits: Astragalus maowensis, also known as Maowensis milkvetch, is a plant that has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine. Research suggests that Astragalus maowensis has various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It may help to boost the immune system and has been used to treat conditions such as fever and digestive issues. Additionally, the plant has been shown to have potential anti-cancer properties and may help to protect against cardiovascular disease. Further studies are needed to fully understand the health benefits of Astragalus maowensis.
parts: Astragalus maowensis, the roots and aerial parts of the plant are used in traditional medicine. The roots are rich in bioactive compounds and have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The plant's aerial parts, including the leaves and stems, are also used to make medicinal preparations. The roots and aerial parts are typically harvested, dried, and processed for use in herbal remedies. The specific medicinal properties and uses of Astragalus maowensis may vary depending on the region and cultural traditions.
